steps.title: Use a two-point regression
steps.content: Treat $h(28)=15$ and $h(26)=22$ as the points $(28,15)$ and $(26,22)$. In Desmos, enter $x_1=[28,26]$, $y_1=[15,22]$, and $y_1\sim mx_1+b$. The regression gives $m=-\frac{7}{2}$ and $b=113$, so $h(x)=-\frac{7}{2}x+113$.

steps.title: Check the slope and intercept
steps.content: By hand, the slope is $\frac{22-15}{26-28}=\frac{7}{-2}=-\frac{7}{2}$. Substitute $(28,15)$ into $h(x)=-\frac{7}{2}x+b$: $15=-98+b$, so $b=113$.

steps.title: Verify and eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
Check: $h(26)=-\frac{7}{2}(26)+113=-91+113=22.$ Correct.
  • $h(x)=-\frac{2}{7}x+23$: slope $-\frac{2}{7}$ does not match $-\frac{7}{2}$ — wrong slope.
  • $h(x)=-\frac{2}{7}x+113$: wrong slope.
  • $h(x)=-\frac{7}{2}x+23$: correct slope but $h(28)=-98+23=-75\neq 15$ — wrong intercept.
  • $h(x)=-\frac{7}{2}x+113$: matches both data points.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
